# playbook_connectors.csv

**Generated by:** [`map_solutions_connectors_tables.py`](../map_solutions_connectors_tables.md)
**Granularity:** One row per `(playbook, api_name, api_kind)`
**Type:** Mapping / edge table

## Overview

Logic App connectors and built-in actions used by playbooks. Each row captures one connector type or built-in action type referenced by a playbook's ARM template:

- **Managed / custom API connections** — derived from `Microsoft.Web/connections` resources (`api_kind` = `managedApi` or `customApi`).
- **Built-in actions** — derived from walking the workflow `definition.actions` tree and capturing `Http`, `Function`, `Workflow`, and `ApiManagement` action types (`api_kind` = `builtin`). Pure control-flow actions (`If`, `Foreach`, `Switch`, `Until`, `Scope`, `InitializeVariable`, `SetVariable`, `Compose`, `ParseJson`, `Terminate`, `Response`, `Recurrence`, etc.) are intentionally excluded.

For each `api_name`, the `parameters` column captures per-action-invocation parameter values (most usefully, the URL of every `Http` call).

This complements [`content_items.csv`](content_items.md) which describes the playbook itself.

## Use Cases

- **Connector usage profiling** — answer "which playbooks use the `azuresentinel` connector?" or "how many playbooks depend on Microsoft Teams?".
- **Custom-connector inventory** — filter `is_custom = true` to find playbooks that ship custom Logic App APIs.
- **Sentinel-aware playbooks** — filter `is_sentinel = true` to find playbooks that read or write Sentinel data via the Microsoft Sentinel connector.
- **HTTP endpoint inventory** — filter `api_kind = builtin` and `api_name = http`, then `parse_json(parameters)` to extract every external URL invoked by playbooks.
- **Function / child-workflow dependency graphs** — `api_name = function` rows expose `functionId`; `api_name = workflow` rows expose `workflowId`.
- **Statistics** — aggregate connector and built-in counts across the entire repository.

## Columns

| Column | Description | Data Source |
|--------|-------------|-------------|
| `solution_name` | Owning solution display name (empty for standalone/GitHub-only playbooks) | Parent solution metadata |
| `solution_folder` | Solution folder name (joinable to `content_items.csv`) | File system |
| `solution_github_url` | GitHub URL to the solution's folder | Computed |
| `playbook_name` | Playbook display name (matches `content_name` in `content_items.csv`) | ARM `metadata.title` / filename |
| `playbook_file` | Path to the playbook ARM template, relative to its `Playbooks` folder (joinable to `content_items.csv` `content_file`) | File system |
| `api_name` | Connector identifier — last segment of `properties.api.id` after `/managedApis/` or `/customApis/` (e.g., `azuresentinel`, `office365`, `teams`, `AbuseIPDBAPI`); for built-ins the lowercase action type (`http`, `function`, `workflow`, `apimanagement`) | ARM template parsing / action walk |
| `api_kind` | `managedApi` (Microsoft-published Logic App connector), `customApi` (custom connector shipped with the solution), or `builtin` (Logic App built-in action) | Parsed from `properties.api.id` / action `type` |
| `is_custom` | `true` when `api_kind == customApi`; otherwise `false` | Derived |
| `is_sentinel` | `true` when `api_name` is `azuresentinel` (Microsoft Sentinel connector) | Derived |
| `connection_count` | Number of `Microsoft.Web/connections` resources within this playbook that reference this `api_name` (always `0` for `builtin` rows) | ARM template scan |
| `connection_kinds` | `;`-separated distinct values of the resource `kind` field across the connection instances (e.g., `V1`, `V2`) | ARM template scan |
| `connection_names` | `;`-separated resource names of the connection instances (often ARM expressions like `[variables('AzureSentinelConnectionName')]`) | ARM template scan |
| `action_count` | Number of action invocations of this connector/built-in within the playbook's workflow `definition.actions` tree (includes `ApiConnection` actions for managed/custom rows) | Workflow walk |
| `parameters` | JSON array (loadable via Kusto `parse_json`) — one object per action invocation. Always includes `action` (the action's name in the workflow). Per-type fields: **Http** → `method`, `uri`; **ApiConnection** (managed/custom) → `method`, `path`; **Function** → `functionId`, `method`; **Workflow** → `workflowId`, `triggerName`; **ApiManagement** → `apiManagementName`, `operationId`, `pathTemplate`, `method`. Empty string when no action-level params were captured. | Workflow walk |

> ARM-expression `id` values such as `/customApis/', parameters('customApis_X_name'))` are resolved by looking up the parameter's `defaultValue` or, as a fallback, by parsing the api name out of the parameter name itself (`customApis_X_name` → `X`).
> URLs and paths in `parameters` may contain Logic App expressions (`@{variables('X')}`, `@{parameters('Y')}`) — they are captured verbatim and not evaluated.

## Related CSVs

- [`content_items.csv`](content_items.md) — playbook metadata; join on `(solution_folder, playbook_file)` ↔ `(solution_folder, content_file)`.
- [`connectors.csv`](connectors.md) — the **data** connectors catalog. This is unrelated to Logic App connectors but the term "connector" is overloaded; see the README for a definition glossary.
